builderCode:
node_panels = {}  # node.val -> Panel, persists across steps

head_label = Label('head', width=2, color=(255, 100, 100))
head_label.visible = False
head_arrow = Arrow(width=2, color=(255, 100, 100), orientation='down')
head_arrow.visible = False

cur_label = Label('cur', width=2, color=(100, 200, 255))
cur_label.visible = False
cur_arrow = Arrow(width=2, color=(100, 200, 255), orientation='down')
cur_arrow.visible = False

def function_exit(function_name, value):
    if function_name == 'Node.__init__' and hasattr(value, 'val'):
        idx = len(node_panels)
        panel = Panel(str(value.val))
        panel.width = 2
        panel.height = 1

        r = Rect()
        r.width = 2
        r.height = 1
        r.color = (80, 140, 220)
        panel.position = (2, idx * 3)
        panel.add(r)

        lbl = Label(str(value.val), width=2, z=-1)
        panel.add(lbl)

        node_panels[value.val] = panel

        if idx > 0:
            arr = Arrow()
            arr.orientation = 'right'
            arr.position = (0, -1)
            panel.add(arr)

def update(params, scope):
    head_obj = params.get('head')
    if head_obj is not None and hasattr(head_obj, 'val') and head_obj.val in node_panels:
        col = node_panels[head_obj.val].position[1]
        head_label.position = (0, col)
        head_arrow.position = (1, col)
        head_label.visible = True
        head_arrow.visible = True
    else:
        head_label.visible = False
        head_arrow.visible = False

    cur_obj = params.get('cur')
    if cur_obj is not None and hasattr(cur_obj, 'val') and cur_obj.val in node_panels:
        col = node_panels[cur_obj.val].position[1]
        cur_label.position = (0, col)
        cur_arrow.position = (1, col)
        cur_label.visible = True
        cur_arrow.visible = True
    else:
        cur_label.visible = False
        cur_arrow.visible = False
debuggerCode:
# Linked list construction — watch nodes appear as each __init__ returns

class Node:
    def __init__(self, val):
        self.val = val
        self.next = None

head = Node(1)
cur = head
for v in [2, 3, 4, 5]:
    node = Node(v)
    cur.next = node
    cur = node
